title: corydalis mira subsp. fokarica
intro: Corydalis mira subsp. fokarica, also known as Fokarikova corydalina, is a subspecies of perennial plant in the poppy family (Papaveraceae) native to the Balkan Peninsula. The plant's rhizome and roots have been traditionally used in folk medicine for their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to the presence of alkaloids such as protopine and allocryptopine. The medicinal properties of Corydalis mira subsp. fokarica have been found to have health benefits, including pain relief, reducing inflammation, and exhibiting potential antimicrobial and antioxidant activities. In herbal preparations, the plant is often combined with other herbs and used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to treat various health conditions, including arthritis, digestive issues, and anxiety.
